chunk_summary: NFHS-5 data highlights India's undernutrition challenges, including stunting, anemia, and inadequate diets. Clinical practice should integrate feeding counselling, growth monitoring, and community systems like ICDS and POSHAN Abhiyaan for effective management.
learning_objectives: Understand NFHS-5 indicators on undernutrition and feeding practices in India.
Learn how to integrate growth monitoring and community systems like ICDS into clinical care.
Recognize the role of micronutrient-focused counselling in addressing anemia and faltering growth.
key_facts: Only 11.3% of children aged 6–23 months receive an adequate diet.
Anemia affects 67.1% of children aged 6–59 months.
Exclusive breastfeeding under 6 months is reported at 63.7%.
ICDS provides supplementary nutrition, growth monitoring, and health education for children under 6.
reference_quote: The ICDS platform delivers supplementary nutrition, growth monitoring, health education, and referral for children under 6 and mothers, and many families receive weights through anganwadi services.
document_key_topics: Feeding difficulties and faltering growth in India
Role of ICDS and POSHAN Abhiyaan in growth monitoring
